intro: Micrampelis minima, also known as Creeping Snowberry, is a small, low-growing shrub native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits of reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Creeping Snowberry include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Creeping Snowberry is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, or dried and used as a powder for capsule or tablet formulation.
